app.filters package

Submodules

app.filters.collection_filters module

The module provides functions for filtering collection attributes, such as collection name and collection summary. These filters are used within Pydantic schemas.

app.filters.collection_filters.filter_collection_name(collection_name: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters a collection name by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty, it returns none. Otherwise, it returns the trimmed collection name.

app.filters.collection_filters.filter_collection_summary(collection_summary: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters a collection summary. If there is no input, or if the input is an empty string or consists only of whitespace, it returns none. Otherwise, it returns the trimmed user signature.

app.filters.comment_filters module

The module provides functions for filtering comment attributes, such as comment content. The validator is used within Pydantic schemas.

app.filters.comment_filters.filter_comment_content(comment_content: str) → str[source]

Filters a comment content by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty, it returns none. Otherwise, it returns the trimmed comment content.

app.filters.document_filters module

The module provides functions for filtering document attributes such as filename, summary, and tags. These filters are used to ensure the input data is cleaned (trimmed and standardized) before being processed further.

app.filters.document_filters.filter_document_filename(document_filename: str) → str[source]

Filters a document name by stripping leading and trailing whitespace.

app.filters.document_filters.filter_document_summary(document_summary: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters a document summary. If the input is empty or consists only of whitespace, it returns None. Otherwise, it returns the trimmed document summary.

app.filters.document_filters.filter_tags(tags: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters tags by stripping leading and trailing whitespace and converting them to lowercase. If the input is empty, it returns None.

app.filters.option_filters module

The module provides functions for filtering option keys and values. These filters are used within Pydantic schemas.

app.filters.option_filters.filter_option_key(option_key: str) → str[source]

Filters an option key by stripping leading and trailing whitespace, and converting it to lowercase.

app.filters.option_filters.filter_option_value(option_value: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters an option value by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the value is empty or None, returns None.

app.filters.partner_filters module

The module provides functions for filtering partner-related attributes. These filters are used within Pydantic schemas.

app.filters.partner_filters.filter_partner_contacts(partner_contacts: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters partner contacts by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty or consists only of whitespace, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed contacts information.

app.filters.partner_filters.filter_partner_name(partner_name: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters a partner name by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed partner name.

app.filters.partner_filters.filter_partner_region(partner_region: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters a partner region by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty or consists only of whitespace, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed partner region.

app.filters.partner_filters.filter_partner_summary(partner_summary: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters a partner summary by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty or consists only of whitespace, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed partner summary.

app.filters.partner_filters.filter_partner_type(partner_type: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters a partner type by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty or consists only of whitespace, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed partner type.

app.filters.partner_filters.filter_partner_website(partner_website: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters a partner website by stripping leading and trailing whitespace, and converting it to lowercase. If the input is empty or consists only of whitespace, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed and lowercase website.

app.filters.user_filters module

The module provides functions for filtering user entity attributes, such as login, password, first name, last name, TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password), and token expiration time. These filters are used within Pydantic schemas.

app.filters.user_filters.filter_first_name(first_name: str) → str[source]

Filters a first name by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed first name.

app.filters.user_filters.filter_last_name(last_name: str) → str[source]

Filters a last name by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed last name.

app.filters.user_filters.filter_user_caption(user_caption: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters a user caption by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty or None, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed user caption.

app.filters.user_filters.filter_user_contacts(user_contacts: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Filters user contacts by stripping leading and trailing whitespace. If the input is empty or None, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed contacts.

app.filters.user_filters.filter_user_login(user_login: str) → str[source]

Filters a user login by stripping leading and trailing whitespace and converting it to lowercase. If the input is empty, returns None. Otherwise, returns the trimmed and lowercase user login.

app.filters.user_filters.filter_user_totp(user_totp: str) → str[source]

Filters a user TOTP by checking if the input is numeric. If not, raises a ValueError. Otherwise, returns the original TOTP.
